---
title: "Day 45 — Milestone 3 - nanochat, part 2: SFT, RL, eval, serving"
subtitle: "Sunday, 6 September 2026 · Week 7 · Pipeline, RAG, evaluation · Milestone · 3-4 h"
---

::: {.day-nav .kind-milestone}
[← Day 44](day-44.qmd) · [All 50 days](../timeline.qmd) · [Day 46 →](day-46.qmd)
:::

::: {.why}
This is the one repo that shows raw text becoming a served chat model with nothing hidden, and today you write the trace that proves you can follow it. Renting an 8xH100 node and running runs/speedrun.sh yourself is optional - about 1.5 h and roughly $48 on-demand or $15 spot. Reading the code carefully gets you the whole picture; running it mostly buys you the experience of watching it happen.
:::

## What you should be able to do tonight

- You can follow one training example from raw web text through tokenization, base pretraining, mid-training, SFT and RL, saying what changes about it at each hop.
- You can explain what the KV cache stores, why decode is cheap relative to prefill once it exists, and what it costs in memory as context grows.
- You can attach a wall-clock figure and a dollar figure to every stage of the speedrun.

## Tasks <span class='task-total'>205 min</span>

::: {.task-list}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [45 min]{.task-min} Read chat_sft.py. Work out the conversation format, which special tokens delimit turns, and exactly which token positions contribute to the loss. Then find where mid-training happens relative to SFT and write two sentences on what mid-training is for that SFT is not.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [40 min]{.task-min} Read chat_rl.py. Identify the task it optimizes, where the reward comes from, and how the update rule differs from both the DPO loss you derived on Day 43 and the PPO setup in InstructGPT. Note why this stage is applied to a narrow task rather than to chat in general.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [35 min]{.task-min} Read chat_eval.py and the leaderboard file in dev/. List the tasks it scores and how each is scored, then put that list next to your Milestone 2 report from Day 35 and mark which of your metrics have no counterpart here and which of theirs have no counterpart in yours.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [40 min]{.task-min} Read chat_cli.py and infer_bench.py. Find where the KV cache is allocated and updated, separate the prefill path from the decode path, and write down the tokens/sec numbers infer_bench.py reports along with what would make each of them worse.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [45 min]{.task-min} Write nanochat_pipeline.md: the full trace from raw text to served model, one section per stage, each with input, output, time, cost, and one sentence naming the day in Weeks 1-6 where you built the same component yourself.
:::
:::

## Resources

- [nanochat](https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat) [repo]{.res-kind}
- [nanochat miniseries v1 (discussion #420)](https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat/discussions/420) [discussion]{.res-kind}
- [Adding abilities - the strawberry guide (discussion #164)](https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat/discussions/164) [discussion]{.res-kind}
- [nanochat leaderboard](https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat/blob/master/dev/LEADERBOARD.md) [leaderboard]{.res-kind}
- [Beating GPT-2 for <<$100 (discussion #481)](https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat/discussions/481) [discussion]{.res-kind}

## Deliverable

::: {.deliverable}
nanochat_pipeline.md - the end-to-end trace, with a cost and time column that sums to the README's headline figure, and a mapping from each stage back to the day you built it yourself.
:::

## Checkpoint

::: {.checkpoint}
In chat_sft.py, which token positions contribute to the loss and which are masked out - and what specifically goes wrong if you train on all of them?
:::

## Prepare for tomorrow

Ten minutes tonight so Day 46, Monday, 7 September 2026 starts clean. This is what the evening email sends you.

::: {.task-list .prep}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Download SLP3 Chapter 11 (11.pdf) and put it where you read PDFs.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Read the RAG paper abstract (arXiv 2005.11401) - just the abstract.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Run pip install sentence-transformers faiss-cpu and confirm both import cleanly.
:::
:::
